canonical_name: Electrolux Steam Oven EB7SL7KCN / EB7SL7KSP — User Manual (Phase 29 starter pack)/Troubleshooting/The internal light does not work/Service and policy
text: Service and policy
F111: Food sensor / steam circuit fault
The appliance displays **F111** when the steam circuit or the food sensor reports an internal fault that prevents safe operation.
Switch the appliance off and disconnect it from the mains.
Wait at least one minute.
Reconnect the mains and switch the appliance on.
If F111 reappears, contact the Authorised Service Centre.
Warning: Do not attempt to repair the steam circuit yourself.

canonical_name: setting_the_water_hardness
text: Setting the water hardness
Press the **Menu** key.
Select **Basic Settings** with the rotary knob.
Select **Water Hardness**.
Choose level I, II, III, or IV per the water-hardness chart.
Confirm with **OK**.

canonical_name: first_connection_initial_cleaning_and_preheat

metadata.title: First connection — initial cleaning and preheat

text: First connection — initial cleaning and preheat
Remove all accessories and packaging.
Wipe the cavity with a soft cloth, warm water, and a mild detergent.
Place the wire shelf and baking tray in the oven.
Set the heating function to **True Fan Cooking** at 230 °C for one hour.
Allow the oven to cool before further use.

canonical_name: filling_the_water_drawer_for_steam_cooking

metadata.title: Filling the water drawer for steam cooking

text: Filling the water drawer for steam cooking
Open the water drawer (top-left of the appliance front).
Fill with cold drinking water up to the **MAX** mark.
Close the drawer until it clicks.
Start the steam heating function from the main menu.

canonical_name: emptying_the_water_drawer
text: Emptying the water drawer
Wait until the cavity has cooled.
Open the water drawer.
Pour the residual water into a sink.
Wipe the drawer with a dry cloth.

canonical_name: starting_a_heating_function
text: Starting a heating function
Press the **On / Off** key.
Press the heating-function key.
Rotate the rotary knob to choose a heating function (True Fan Cooking, Conventional Cooking, Grill, Steam, etc.).
Press the temperature key and rotate the knob to set the temperature.
Press **OK** to confirm.

canonical_name: steam_cooking
text: Steam cooking
Fill the water drawer.
Place the food on the wire shelf or steam set.
Press the heating-function key and select a steam function (Steam, Steam Regenerate, etc.).
Set temperature and time.
Press **OK** to start.

canonical_name: using_the_food_sensor_for_meat

metadata.title: Using the food sensor for meat

text: Using the food sensor for meat
Insert the food-sensor tip into the centre of the meat.
Connect the sensor plug to the socket on the side wall of the cavity.
Select a heating function.
Set the core temperature on the display.
Press **OK**. The appliance signals when the core temperature is reached.

canonical_name: steam_cleaning_the_cavity
text: Steam cleaning the cavity
Empty the cavity.
Fill the water drawer.
Open the Cleaning submenu.
Select **Steam Cleaning**.
Press **OK**. The function runs for the indicated time.
After the cycle ends, wipe the cavity with a soft cloth.

canonical_name: descaling_the_steam_circuit
text: Descaling the steam circuit
Empty the water drawer.
Fill the drawer with descaling solution per manufacturer instructions.
Open the Cleaning submenu and select **Descaling**.
Follow the on-screen prompts; the cycle takes around 30 minutes.
Rinse twice with cold water to remove residual descaler.

canonical_name: drying_the_cavity_after_steam_cooking

metadata.title: Drying the cavity after steam cooking

text: Drying the cavity after steam cooking
After a steam cycle finishes, empty the water drawer.
Open the door slightly and let the cavity cool.
Wipe the cavity walls and floor with a soft cloth.
Leave the door open for 30 minutes to ensure full drying.

canonical_name: number_of_shelf_positions
text: Number of shelf positions: 5

Warning: The appliance and its accessible parts become hot
during use. Care should be taken to avoid touching heating
elements.

Warning: Always use oven gloves to remove or put in
accessories or ovenware.

Warning: Before carrying out any maintenance, disconnect the
appliance from the power supply.

Warning: Children of less than 3 years of age should be kept
away unless continuously supervised. Keep children and pets
away from the appliance when it operates or when it cools
down.
Warning: Do not use a steam cleaner to clean the appliance.

Warning: Do not use harsh abrasive cleaners or sharp metal
scrapers to clean the glass door, since they can scratch the
surface and the glass may shatter.

Hazard: Risk of steam burn — hot steam may escape when the
oven door is opened during or after a steam cooking
programme. Open the door carefully.

Hazard: Risk of electric shock — only a qualified person must
install this appliance and replace the cable.

Warning: The food sensor becomes hot during use. Use oven
gloves to remove the food sensor.

Warning: Do not replace the internal light yourself. Contact
the Authorised Service Centre.

**Scope note.** This Markdown pack is a *Phase 29 scaffolded
starter* — it captures the highest-value safety, procedure,
control, error-code, spec, and troubleshooting content from the
real Electrolux Steam Oven manual to demonstrate that the
`manual-graph-rag` architecture works on real PDF text. Full
content authoring (every cooking-time table, every cleaning
sub-step, every variant per model) is the Phase 28E
Workstream-1 ask. Source PDF is local-only under
`data_local/real_manuals/raw/electrolux_steam_oven/`.

The food sensor becomes hot during use. Use oven gloves and be careful when unplugging the food sensor or removing it from the food. Touching the food sensor, the core temperature sensor, or the meat probe immediately after cooking can cause burns.
The heating element and the oven cavity walls become hot during use. Do not touch the heating element while the oven is in operation. Use oven gloves to insert or remove accessories.
Do not attempt to repair the steam circuit yourself. Contact the Authorised Service Centre.
Do not bypass the safety interlock under any circumstances. The interlock is part of the steam circuit protection and may only be inspected by an Authorised Service Centre.
The control panel contains:
**Rotary knob** — used to select functions and adjust values.
**Display** — shows the current function, temperature, time,
and prompts.
**On / Off key** — switches the appliance on and off.
**Menu key** — opens the main menu (functions, settings,
cleaning).
**OK key** — confirms a selection.
**Back key** — returns to the previous menu.
**Wire shelf** — supports cookware and trays at various
positions.
**Baking tray** — for baking and roasting on the shelf.
**Grill / roasting pan** — for grilling and collecting
drippings.
**Food sensor** — inserts into food; the appliance reads the
core temperature.
**Steam set** — perforated and unperforated trays for steam
cooking.
Pyrolytic functions are not available on this model.
Possible cause: the heating function and temperature have not been confirmed.
Remedy: press the heating-function key, then the temperature key, then **OK**.
The steam circuit or food sensor reports a fault.
Remedy: switch off, wait one minute, switch on again. If F111 persists, contact the Authorised Service Centre.
Possible causes: the water drawer is empty, the drawer is not fully closed, or the appliance has not yet reached the steam temperature.
Remedy: fill and close the drawer; wait for the temperature to stabilise.
Possible cause: the sensor plug is not fully inserted into the socket on the side wall of the cavity.
Remedy: unplug, wait a few seconds, and insert again until it clicks.
Remedy: do not replace the internal light yourself. Contact the Authorised Service Centre.
All cabling and electrical repairs must be carried out by an
Authorised Service Centre.
The food sensor and steam set are the only user-replaceable
accessories; all internal components are service-only.
Disposal must follow local electrical-appliance regulations.
